topics.title: Why every AI system is built on numbers
Scalars — a single number representing one property
Vectors — a list of numbers representing multiple properties at once
Matrices — grids of numbers and how AI uses them
A vector as a point in space — music taste described as three numbers
Dot product as similarity — vectors pointing the same direction score high
Embeddings — turning words into vectors that capture meaning
Why 'king − man + woman ≈ queen' works
Cosine similarity — measuring the angle between two meaning-vectors
Using the TensorFlow Embedding Projector to explore word clusters
concepts_introduced: reading nearest neighbours in the Projector

topics.title: Probability basics — likelihood, events, outcomes
Conditional probability — P(A given B)
Bayes' theorem intuition — updating belief with new evidence
Why LLMs output a probability distribution, not a single fixed answer
Temperature — low picks the most likely token, high introduces variation
Mean, median, mode — measuring consistency across AI runs
Accuracy — what proportion of outputs were correct
Precision — of the things the model flagged, how many were actually correct
Recall — of all the things that were correct, how many did the model find
Confusion matrix — why 95% accuracy can still mean frequent failure
Interpreting AI output variation — what it tells you about reliability
Embedding explorer — comparing domain-specific word clusters in 2D
Similarity scoring — computing cosine similarity between sentence pairs
Precision vs recall trade-off — when does recall matter more than precision?
F1 score — balancing precision and recall into one metric
concepts_introduced: single-number metric vs reporting precision/recall separately

topics.title: Computing accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 by hand from a confusion matrix

test set size for a trustworthy (non-noisy) score

topics.title: System 1 thinking — fast, instinctive, automatic

Automation complacency — how high accuracy makes humans less vigilant
